content:
Identify the agent with the fewest transfer counts in the last 4 quarters among those who handled more than 0 cases. Return only the Id of the agent.

## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-04-10

message.content:
Identify the agent with the fewest transfer counts in the last 4 quarters among those who handled more than 0 cases. Return only the Id of the agent.

## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-04-10

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Find agent with fewest transfers last 4 quarters

input.command: psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U shreyashankar -d crm_support -c "SELECT * FROM casehistory__c WHERE field__c='Owner Assignment' LIMIT 10;"
psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U shreyashankar -d crm_support -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM casehistory__c WHERE field__c='Owner Assignment' AND oldvalue__c IS NOT NULL AND oldvalue__c <> '';"
psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U shreyashankar -d crm_support -c "SELECT caseid__c, COUNT(*) FROM casehistory__c WHERE field__c='Owner Assignment' GROUP BY caseid__c HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 LIMIT 5;"

input.description: Check one case with multiple owners

input.command:
psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U shreyashankar -d crm_support -c "
WITH last_4q AS (
  SELECT * FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c='Owner Assignment' 
    AND createddate::timestamp >= '2022-04-01' 
    AND createddate::timestamp < '2023-04-01'
),
handled AS (
  -- agents who handled any case (initial or via transfer) in last 4 quarters
  SELECT DISTINCT newvalue__c AS agent FROM last_4q WHERE newvalue__c IS NOT NULL AND newvalue__c <> ''
),
transfers AS (
  -- transfers originating from agent in last 4 quarters
  SELECT oldvalue__c AS agent, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count
  FROM last_4q
  WHERE oldvalue__c IS NOT NULL AND oldvalue__c <> ''
  GROUP BY oldvalue__c
)
SELECT h.agent, COALESCE(t.transfer_count, 0) AS tc
FROM handled h
LEFT JOIN transfers t ON h.agent = t.agent
ORDER BY tc ASC, h.agent
LIMIT 20;
"

lastPrompt: Identify the agent with the fewest transfer counts in the last 4 quarters among those who handled more than 0 cases. Return only the Id of the agent.  ## Transfer Count Policy - Definition: The number…

input.command:
psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U shreyashankar -d crm_support -c "
SELECT oldvalue__c, createddate 
FROM casehistory__c 
WHERE field__c='Owner Assignment' 
  AND oldvalue__c IS NOT NULL 
  AND oldvalue__c <> ''
ORDER BY createddate;
"

psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U shreyashankar -d crm_support -c "
-- All cases with multiple owner assignments
SELECT caseid__c, createddate, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c
FROM casehistory__c
WHERE field__c='Owner Assignment'
  AND caseid__c IN (
    SELECT caseid__c FROM casehistory__c WHERE field__c='Owner Assignment' GROUP BY caseid__c HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
  )
ORDER BY caseid__c, createddate;
"

psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U shreyashankar -d crm_support -c "
WITH last_4q AS (
  SELECT * FROM casehistory__c 
  WHERE field__c='Owner Assignment' 
    AND createddate::timestamp >= '2022-04-01' 
    AND createddate::timestamp < '2023-04-01'
),
handled AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT newvalue__c AS agent FROM last_4q WHERE newvalue__c IS NOT NULL AND newvalue__c <> ''
),
transfers AS (
  SELECT oldvalue__c AS agent, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count
  FROM last_4q
  WHERE oldvalue__c IS NOT NULL AND oldvalue__c <> ''
  GROUP BY oldvalue__c
),
cases_handled AS (
  SELECT agent, COUNT(DISTINCT caseid__c) AS case_count
  FROM (
    SELECT newvalue__c AS agent, caseid__c FROM last_4q
    WHERE newvalue__c IS NOT NULL AND newvalue__c <> ''
  ) s
  GROUP BY agent
)
SELECT h.agent, COALESCE(t.transfer_count, 0) AS tc, c.case_count
FROM handled h
LEFT JOIN transfers t ON h.agent = t.agent
LEFT JOIN cases_handled c ON h.agent = c.agent
ORDER BY tc ASC, c.case_count DESC;
"
input.description: Transfer counts + case counts for all agents

lastPrompt: Identify the agent with the fewest transfer counts in the last 4 quarters among those who handled more than 0 cases. Return only the Id of the agent.  ## Transfer Count Policy - Definition: The number…

input.command:
psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U shreyashankar -d crm_support -c "
-- Filter by case created date in last 4 quarters
WITH cases_last_4q AS (
  SELECT id FROM \"case\" 
  WHERE createddate::timestamp >= '2022-04-01' 
    AND createddate::timestamp < '2023-04-01'
),
oa AS (
  SELECT ch.* FROM casehistory__c ch
  JOIN cases_last_4q c ON ch.caseid__c = c.id
  WHERE ch.field__c='Owner Assignment'
),
transfers AS (
  SELECT oldvalue__c AS agent, COUNT(*) AS tc
  FROM oa WHERE oldvalue__c IS NOT NULL AND oldvalue__c <> ''
  GROUP BY oldvalue__c
),
handled AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT newvalue__c AS agent FROM oa WHERE newvalue__c <> '' AND newvalue__c IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT h.agent, COALESCE(t.tc, 0) AS transfer_count
FROM handled h
LEFT JOIN transfers t ON h.agent = t.agent
ORDER BY transfer_count, h.agent
LIMIT 40;
"
